title: WorkflowProfile
description: The work-order lifecycle expressed as DATA, not code: an ordered set of states and the guarded transitions between them. A WorkOrder.status is one of the profile's state keys; a WorkOrderType may name a workflowProfile (null = the org default) so, e.g., reactive breakdowns keep the full path while administrative work skips review/approval. Plantree seeds one default profile (see work-management-lifecycle doc 05); orgs may re-order states, add states (e.g. a 'Parked' backlog) or relax guards without a code change. One JSON document = one profile.

key.description: Stable key referenced by WorkOrderType.workflowProfile, e.g. 'default'.

isDefault.description: True for the org's default profile, used when a WorkOrderType names no profile.

states.description: The lifecycle states, in display order. A WorkOrder.status must be one of these keys.

transitions.description: The allowed moves between states. A transition not listed here is forbidden; the transitions endpoint enforces guards before applying one.

key.description: Stable state key, e.g. 'in_progress'. Referenced by WorkOrder.status and by transitions.

category.description: Coarse grouping for boards, dashboards and metrics. 'open' = not yet started (requested..assigned); 'active' = work underway; 'on_hold' = paused; 'done' = completed/verified/closed; 'rejected' = terminal without work.

isInitial.description: A state work may enter the machine at. Requested is initial for demand; a PM-generated order may enter at Planned/Scheduled.

isTerminal.description: An end state (Closed, Rejected). No transitions leave a terminal state; work in it is immutable.

pausesSla.description: While in this state the due/SLA clock is paused (e.g. Awaiting parts/access).

label.description: Verb shown on the action, e.g. 'Approve', 'Start work'.

guards.description: Conditions that must hold before the transition is allowed. Empty = always permitted.

actions.description: Side effects fired when the transition succeeds (notify, reserve/deduct parts, create follow-up, webhook, run automation).

type.description: The guard kind. Mirrors the default transition rules in doc 05 (e.g. 'role' + a required role to approve; 'actuals_captured' before InProgress -> Completed).
The side effect to run on this transition.

target.description: Optional target for the action, e.g. a role/team to notify or a webhook key.
